title: triglochin bulbosa subsp. laxiflora
intro: Triglochin bulbosa subsp. laxiflora, also known as the New Zealand drimia or more commonly as the 'narrow-leaved drimia', is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaginaceae. This plant is said to have medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are used to treat various health conditions. The roots of Triglochin bulbosa subsp. laxiflora contain inulin, a type of starch that is known to have health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels and aiding in weight loss. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas or infusions, and it is also used in some traditional medicine practices due to its reported health benefits.
